steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content: Every term in $17x^3 + 2x^2 + 7x$ contains at least one factor of $x$. The GCF is $x$.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The result $x(17x^2 + 2x + 7)$ matches the correct answer exactly.

explanation:
• Choice A — $x(x^2 + 2x + 7)$: Expanding gives $x^3 + 2x^2 + 7x$, missing the coefficient 17 on the cubic term. ✗
  • Choice B — $x(17x^3 + 2x^2 + 1)$: Expanding gives $17x^4 + 2x^3 + x$, which changes the degree and loses the 7x term. ✗
  • Choice C — $x(17x^2 + 2x + 7)$: Expanding gives $17x^3 + 2x^2 + 7x$, which is exactly the original expression. ✓
  • Choice D — $26\left(x^3 + x^2 + x\right)$: Expanding gives $26x^3 + 26x^2 + 26x$; the coefficients 17, 2, 7 are gone and replaced by 26. ✗
C is correct.
